growth_records.medium: Synthetic mineral medium with D-galacturonate, tryptone and yeast extract (anaerobic)

up.method: batch OD/HPLC (rate not numerically reported for batch; only mu given)

sec.units: near-equimolar with lactate (numeric batch rate not reported)
near-equimolar with acetate (numeric batch rate not reported)

growth_records.cite: Valk LC et al. A Novel D-Galacturonate Fermentation Pathway in Lactobacillus suebicus Links Initial Reactions of the Galacturonate-Isomerase Route With the Phosphoketolase Pathway. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.03027. [PMC6978723]
growth_records.method: reported (specific growth rate on D-galacturonate); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.20=3.47 h
growth_records.snippet: In anaerobic bioreactor batch cultures, L. suebicus LCV1 exhibited a specific growth rate on D-galacturonate of 0.20 h-1 while acetate and lactate, the sole organic fermentation products, were formed at near-equimolar amounts.
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor, pH 4, 30C, anaerobic, 3.3 g/L D-galacturonate (Fig.2 caption). mu=0.20 h-1 reported. Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.20=3.47 h. Numeric biomass-specific batch rates not tabulated (chemostat rates in separate record). Base medium recipe lists galacturonate 4.3 g/L; batch experiment used 3.3 g/L per Fig.2.

growth_records.cite: Valk LC et al. A Novel D-Galacturonate Fermentation Pathway in Lactobacillus suebicus Links Initial Reactions of the Galacturonate-Isomerase Route With the Phosphoketolase Pathway. Frontiers in Microbiology (2020). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.03027. [PMC6978723]
growth_records.method: calculated (mu=D at steady state = 0.13 h-1); td=ln2/0.13=5.33 h
growth_records.snippet: biomass-specific production rates of acetate and lactate were 6.0 +/- 0.1 and 5.2 +/- 0.1 mmol gx-1 h-1... biomass yield on D-galacturonate in these anaerobic chemostat cultures was 0.09 +/- 0.0 g biomass (g galacturonate)-1. Table 2: Galac
growth_records.notes: Table 2, anaerobic galacturonate-limited chemostat, D=0.13 h-1, pH 4, 30C. mu=D=0.13 h-1 (steady state). td=ln2/0.13=5.33 h. Directly reported biomass-specific rates (mmol/gDW/h): qGalUA=-6.9, qAcetate=+6.0, qLactate=+5.2, qCO2=+7.2. Molar product yields per galacturonate: acetate 0.87, lactate 0.75, CO2 1.04; biomass yield 0.09 g/g. Values are mean +/- avg deviation of two independent steady-state chemostats.
